samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="S0">first state</param>
        <param id="S1">second state</param>
        <param id="S2">third state</param>
        <param id="S3">fourth state</param>
        <param id="S4">fifth state</param>
        <param id="S5">sixth state</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">counter to count the number of cycles.</reg>
        <reg id="start_cnt" type="sequential_logic">start_cnt to control the start of the division.</reg>
        <reg id="res_valid" type="combinational_logic">res_valid to control the output valid.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_temp" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[7:0]">temp to store the divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="dividend_temp" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[7:0]">temp to store the dividend.</reg>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[8:0]">negated divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="sr" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[16:0]">shift register to store the result.</reg>
        <wire id="sub_result" width_description="[8:0]">sub_result to store the result of subtraction.</wire>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    <p1>Operation Start:</p1>
    <p2>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.</p2>
    <p3>Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.</p3>
    <p4>Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.</p4>
    <p5>Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.</p5>
    <p6>Division Process:</p6>
    <p7>If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:</p7>
    <p8>cnt and start_cnt are cleared.</p8>
    <p9>Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.</p9>
    <p10>Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:</p10>
    <p11>Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.</p11>
    <p12>Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.</p12>
    <p13>Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.</p13>
    <p14>Result Validity:</p14>
    <p15>res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.</p15>
    <p16>`res_valid` should be update directly, without passing middle register value.</p16>
    <p17>`res_valid` is set to zero if `rst` is high.</p17>
    <p18>Otherwise, if `cnt` reaches 8, `res_valid` is set to 1.</p18>
    <p19>Otherwise, if `res_valid` and `res_ready` is all high, `res_valid` is set to 0.</p19>
    <p20>Otherwise, `res_valid` is set to `res_valid`.</p20>
    </implementation>
</module>
